mentions.note: Most money under management of any company on earth; emphasizing new fund types; large money repository results expected to be over pretty good number. May not be enough to outperform banks in sector.
Managing astounding $14 trillion in assets; blowout quarter with strong inflows; rallied on earnings beat and market leadership.

Stock -4% on $3.8B redemption news despite honoring requests (bullish signal); private credit funds solvent but narrative risk dominates; employees buying dip is positive; hold but upside capped in credit business.
CEO Jack Dorsey cut workforce 40% (10k→6k) for AI productivity; stock rallied low-50s to high-60s. Blueprint for Big Tech; expects mass AI-driven layoffs industry-wide.
Preferred over Blue Owl in private equity/credit space; 5% yield, better-run management, higher quality.

CEO Jack Dorsey firing 4,000/10,000 (40%) using AI as rationale; represents thesis that AI enables smaller teams; Cramer worries this is failure of imagination vs. Jensen Huang's 'do more with more' approach.
Up $7 today (from ~$4 crisis lows); private equity credit crisis appears limited or 'cornered off'; short squeeze reflects contained damage risk.
